title: The Unriggable Ticket
tagline: A scratch-off printed today, whose winner is chosen by a number nobody on Earth knows yet.
holy_shit: The ticket is printed BEFORE the deciding number exists — a global randomness beacon flips it next round — so the printer literally cannot know the outcome, cannot bias it, and cannot pre-print only losers. No house, no server, no RNG to seed. You scan after the round drops and your own phone recomputes the result byte-for-byte, catching any cheat. Provably-fair gambling with nobody in the middle.
powered_by: /track carries a committed seed + a future drand round + the pinned oracle cell; a verify-before-exec compute cell fetches the CORS-open drand League-of-Entropy beacon and computes outcome = SHA-256(seed ':' round-randomness) mod n — all in the scanner's browser via native crypto.subtle.
agent_scenario: When you need a tamper-proof outcome that no party can bias or predict and no server hosts: commit the inputs up front (printed / in a link), bind the result to a FUTURE public randomness beacon (drand League of Entropy), and let the verifier recompute it byte-for-byte in their own browser through a hash-pinned cell. Reusable for provably-fair draws, unriggable raffles, commit-reveal, fair coin-flips, and any 'trust me' that should be 'verify me instead'.
author: kody-w + Claude
